measurement.name: UVA
measurement.description: Ultraviolet A is a long-wave, black light, not absorbed by the ozone layer: soft UV.

measurement.name: UVB
measurement.description: Ultraviolet is electromagnetic radiation shorter than that of visible light but longer than X-rays. UV radiation is present in sunlight, and constitutes about 10% of the total electromagnetic radiation output from the Sun.Of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the Earth's surface, more than 95% is the longer wavelengths of UVA, with the small remainder UVB. The ozone layer blocks most UVB. UVB is also responsible for the formation of vitamin D in most land vertebrates, including humans.

measurement.name: UVC
measurement.description: Ultraviolet is electromagnetic radiation shorter than that of visible light but longer than X-rays. UV radiation is present in sunlight, and constitutes about 10% of the total electromagnetic radiation output from the Sun.Of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the Earth's surface, more than 95% is the longer wavelengths of UVA, with the small remainder UVB, and almost no UVC reaches the Earth's surface. The ozone layer blocks most UVB and remaining UVC not already blocked by ordinary oxygen in air.

measurement.name: battery
measurement.description: The SCK remaining battery level in percentage.

measurement.name: Barometric Pressure
measurement.description: Barometric pressure is the pressure within the atmosphere of Earth. In most circumstances atmospheric pressure is closely approximated by the hydrostatic pressure caused by the weight of air above the measurement point.

measurement.name: Light
measurement.description: Lux is a measure of how much light is spread over a given area. A full moon clear night is around 1 lux, inside an office building you usually have 400 lux and a bright day can be more than 20000 lux.

measurement.name: SD-Card Presence
measurement.description: This value is 1 if there is a micro SD-card inserted in the kit, and will be 0 otherwise (or if it's corrupted)

measurement.name: PM1
measurement.description: PM stands for particulate matter: a mixture of solid particles and liquid droplets found in the air. PM1 refers to particulate matter < 1um.

measurement.name: PM10
measurement.description: PM stands for particulate matter: a mixture of solid particles and liquid droplets found in the air. PM10 refers to particulate matter < 10um.

measurement.name: PM2.5
measurement.description: PM stands for particulate matter: a mixture of solid particles and liquid droplets found in the air. PM2.5 refers to particulate matter < 2.5um.

measurement.name: PM4.0
measurement.description: PM stands for particulate matter: the term for a mixture of solid particles and liquid droplets found in the air. Some particles, such as dust, dirt, soot, or smoke, are large or dark enough to be seen with the naked eye.

measurement.description: Particle Number Concentration from previous size to 0.5um

measurement.name: Relative Humidity
measurement.description: Relative humidity is a measure of the amount of moisture in the air relative to the total amount of moisture the air can hold. For instance, if the relative humidity was 50%, then the air is only half saturated with moisture.

measurement.name: Air Temperature
measurement.description: Air temperature is a measure of how hot or cold the air is. It is the most commonly measured weather parameter. Air temperature is dependent on the amount and strength of the sunlight hitting the earth, and atmospheric conditions, such as cloud cover and humidity, which trap heat.

measurement.name: Light

name: Smart Parks1
description: Smart Citizen Kit 2.1 with Urban Sensor Board

sensors.name: AMS CCS811 - TVOC
sensors.description: Total Volatile Organic Compounds Digital Indoor Sensor

measurement.name: TVOC
measurement.description: Total volatile organic compounds is a grouping of a wide range of organic chemical compounds to simplify reporting when these are present in ambient air or emissions. Many substances, such as natural gas, could be classified as volatile organic compounds (VOCs).

sensors.name: AMS CCS811 - eCO2
sensors.description: Equivalent Carbon Dioxide Digital Indoor Sensor

measurement.name: eCO2
measurement.description: Equivalent CO2 is the concentration of CO2 that would cause the same level of radiative forcing as a given type and concentration of greenhouse gas. Examples of such greenhouse gases are methane, perfluorocarbons, and nitrous oxide. CO2 is primarily a by-product of human metabolism and is constantly being emitted into the indoor environment by building occupants. CO2 may come from combustion sources as well. Associations of higher indoor carbon dioxide concentrations with impaired work performance and increased health symptoms have been attributed to correlation of indoor CO2 with concentrations of other indoor air pollutants that are also influenced by rates of outdoor-air ventilation.

measurement.name: UVA
measurement.description: Ultraviolet A is a long-wave, black light, not absorbed by the ozone layer: soft UV.

measurement.name: UVB
measurement.description: Ultraviolet is electromagnetic radiation shorter than that of visible light but longer than X-rays. UV radiation is present in sunlight, and constitutes about 10% of the total electromagnetic radiation output from the Sun.Of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the Earth's surface, more than 95% is the longer wavelengths of UVA, with the small remainder UVB. The ozone layer blocks most UVB. UVB is also responsible for the formation of vitamin D in most land vertebrates, including humans.

measurement.name: UVC
measurement.description: Ultraviolet is electromagnetic radiation shorter than that of visible light but longer than X-rays. UV radiation is present in sunlight, and constitutes about 10% of the total electromagnetic radiation output from the Sun.Of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the Earth's surface, more than 95% is the longer wavelengths of UVA, with the small remainder UVB, and almost no UVC reaches the Earth's surface. The ozone layer blocks most UVB and remaining UVC not already blocked by ordinary oxygen in air.

measurement.description: Nitrogen dioxide is a toxic gas. It is a pollutant in some urban areas due the excess air required for complete combustion of fuels introduces nitrogen into the combustion of internal combustion engines (cars, trucks, ships...) and power stations. ⚠ Note: values are offered in RS (sensor resistance in kOhm) as a relative measurement from the R0 (sensor zero resistance). sensors.name: AMS CCS811 - TVOC
sensors.description: Total Volatile Organic Compounds Digital Indoor Sensor

measurement.name: TVOC
measurement.description: Total volatile organic compounds is a grouping of a wide range of organic chemical compounds to simplify reporting when these are present in ambient air or emissions. Many substances, such as natural gas, could be classified as volatile organic compounds (VOCs).

sensors.name: AMS CCS811 - eCO2
sensors.description: Equivalent Carbon Dioxide Digital Indoor Sensor

measurement.name: eCO2
measurement.description: Equivalent CO2 is the concentration of CO2 that would cause the same level of radiative forcing as a given type and concentration of greenhouse gas. Examples of such greenhouse gases are methane, perfluorocarbons, and nitrous oxide. CO2 is primarily a by-product of human metabolism and is constantly being emitted into the indoor environment by building occupants. CO2 may come from combustion sources as well. Associations of higher indoor carbon dioxide concentrations with impaired work performance and increased health symptoms have been attributed to correlation of indoor CO2 with concentrations of other indoor air pollutants that are also influenced by rates of outdoor-air ventilation.

measurement.description: Lux is a measure of how much light is spread over a given area. A full moon clear night is around 1 lux, inside an office building you usually have 400 lux and a bright day can be more than 20000 lux.

measurement.name: Relative Humidity
measurement.description: Relative humidity is a measure of the amount of moisture in the air relative to the total amount of moisture the air can hold. For instance, if the relative humidity was 50%, then the air is only half saturated with moisture.

measurement.name: Air Temperature
measurement.description: Air temperature is a measure of how hot or cold the air is. It is the most commonly measured weather parameter. Air temperature is dependent on the amount and strength of the sunlight hitting the earth, and atmospheric conditions, such as cloud cover and humidity, which trap heat.

measurement.name: Noise Level
measurement.description: Sound pressure level (SPL) or sound level is a logarithmic measure of the effective sound pressure of a sound relative to a reference value. It is measured in decibels (dB) above a standard reference level.

measurement.name: co
measurement.description: Carbon monoxide is a colorless, odorless, and tasteless gas. It is a pollutant in some urban areas coming from the exhaust of internal combustion engines  (cars, trucks, ships...)  and incomplete combustion of various other fuels. ⚠ Note: values are offered in RS (sensor resistance in kOhm) as a relative measurement from the R0 (sensor zero resistance), we approximate R0 at 75kOhm.

measurement.name: no2
measurement.description: Nitrogen dioxide is a toxic gas. It is a pollutant in some urban areas due the excess air required for complete combustion of fuels introduces nitrogen into the combustion of internal combustion engines (cars, trucks, ships...) and power stations. ⚠ Note: values are offered in RS (sensor resistance in kOhm) as a relative measurement from the R0 (sensor zero resistance). Due to sensor intra-model variability we recommend using this as a relative measurement within the same kit readings.
